title: Bipolar & Lithium Mastery
content: • Dx: Manic Episode: Sleeping little (>1wk), pressured speech. • Risk: Lithium Toxicity: Precipitated by Advil (NSAIDs). Use Aspirin. • Tx: Dialysis if Level >4.

title: Personality Disorders
content:
• Cluster A (Weird): Paranoid, Schizoid, Schizotypal.
• Cluster B (Wild): Antisocial, Borderline, Histrionic.
• Cluster C (Worried): Avoidant, OCPD.

title: APGAR & Birth Injuries
content: • Dx: APGAR: 1 min (tolerance), 5 min (resuscitation). NOT a neuro predictor. • Dx: Erb-Duchenne (C5-C6): Waiters tip position. • Tx: Clavicular Fracture: No tx needed.

title: CHA2DS2-VASc Score | Stroke Risk
content:
• Dx: Used for Atrial Fibrillation anticoagulation.
1 Point: CHF, HTN, DM, Vascular disease, Age 65-74, Female.
2 Points: Age ≥75, Prior Stroke/TIA.
• Tx: Score ≥2 (Male) or ≥3 (Female) → Start Anticoagulants.

title: Centor Score | Strep Pharyngitis
content:
• Dx: Criteria to treat sore throat.
Cough?NONE (1 pt)
Exudates?YES (1 pt)
Nodes?Tender LN (1 pt)
Fever?>38°C (1 pt)
• Tx: Score 4+ → Penicillin. Score 2-3 → Rapid Test.

title: Master Screening Guidelines (MCCQE1)
content: • Colon Cancer: Age 50-75 (Colonoscopy q10y). • Breast Cancer: Mammogram starts @40 (Yearly). • Cervical Cancer: Pap Smear Ages 21-65 (q3y). • Lung Cancer: Age 55 (Low-dose CT) if 30 pack-year smoker. • AAA: Age 65 (One-time US) for male smokers. • DEXA Scan: Starts @65 for Osteoporosis.

title: LAME Rule for Cephalosporins
content:
• Warning: Cephalosporins do NOT cover:
L - Listeria | A - Atypicals | M - MRSA | E - Enterococcus

content: • Colon Cancer: Age 50-75 (Colonoscopy q10y). • Breast Cancer: Mammogram starts @40 (Yearly). • Cervical Cancer: Pap Smear Ages 21-65 (q3y). • Lung Cancer: Age 55 (Low-dose CT) if 30 pack-year smoker. • AAA: Age 65 (One-time US) for male smokers. • DEXA Scan: Starts @65 for Osteoporosis.

title: LAME Rule for Cephalosporins
content:
• Warning: Cephalosporins do NOT cover:
L - Listeria | A - Atypicals | M - MRSA | E - Enterococcus

title: Abdominal Pain – Red Flags
content:
• Weight loss → GI Malignancy • Pain to back → Pancreatitis / AAA • Pain central then RLQ → Appendicitis • Pain to groin (male) → Testicular Torsion / Renal Colic • Blood PR → GI bleed / Malignancy • Shock → Perforated viscus / Hemorrhage / Sepsis
IBS (Manning ≥3): Pain relief with BM · Loose stools with pain · Mucus · Incomplete evacuation · Abdominal distension

title: Anxiety Disorders – Types & Treatment
content:
• GAD: Worry >6 months + fatigue + irritability + insomnia • Panic Disorder: Unexpected episodes, peak at 10 min • PTSD: Trauma → flashbacks + nightmares + avoidance • OCD: Obsessions (thoughts) + Compulsions (rituals)
Tx: GAD → SSRI/SNRI + CBT
Phobia/PD → SSRI + prn anxiolytics
PTSD/OCD → SSRI/TCA + CBT
CBT: 6-8 sessions over 8-12 weeks

title: Asthma – Diagnosis & Management
content: • Dx: Airflow obstruction + Reversibility + No alternative Dx • Good control: No daytime sx, FEV1 >90%, <4 SABA/wk • First-line: PRN SABA (salbutamol) + ICS for ALL • Step up: Increase ICS → Add LABA or Leukotriene antagonist • Exacerbation: Prednisone 50mg x5d (adults) | 1-2mg/kg x5d (kids) • Emergency: O2 + Salbutamol + Steroids if SaO2 <94% ± Ipratropium ± MgSO4

title: Cancer Screening – Canadian Task Force
content:
✅ Breast: Mammography q2-3y → Age 50-74
✅ Cervical: PAP q3y → Age 25-69
✅ Colorectal: FIT q2y OR Flex Sig q10y → Age 50-74
✅ Lung: LDCT Annual x3y → Age 50-74 + ≥30 pack-year
❌ Ovarian: Pelvic exam NOT recommended
⚠️ Prostate PSA: Weak recommendation Age 55-69 only

title: Chest Pain – Emergency Diagnoses
content: • STEMI: ASA + O2 + Nitro + Morphine + β-blocker + Heparin → PCI • PE Wells Score: >6 High risk → CT Pulm Angio | Low/Mod → D-Dimer first • Pericarditis: ASA first-line → NSAIDs → Steroids • Aortic Dissection: CXR + ECG + TEE/CT → Urgent surgical consult + BP control • Pneumothorax: Heimlich valve | Chest tube if >20% collapse

title: Conjunctivitis – Types & Treatment
content:
• Viral: Tearing, bilateral, pre-auricular LN, self-limited (Adenovirus) • Allergic: Itching, stringy discharge, bilateral, seasonal • Bacterial: Crusty discharge throughout day, lid sticking • Hyperacute <24hr → Gonorrhea → urgent Rx!
Tx: Viral: cold compresses | Allergic: Olopatadine 0.1% | Bacterial adults: Moxifloxacin QID x7-10d | Peds: Ciprofloxacin 0.5% QID
❌ NEVER steroids alone

title: Gastroenteritis – Management
content:
• MCC: Rotavirus (kids), Norovirus (adults) • Bloody diarrhea → invasive pathogen → stool culture
Dehydration Tx:
None/Mild → ORT (Pedialyte) 20mL/kg/hr
Severe → 0.9% NaCl 20mL/kg IV bolus x3
• Ondansetron: 0.15mg/kg PO once (>6 months) • Antibiotics ONLY for: Shigella, C.diff, S.typhi, E.histolytica

title: Otitis Media (AOM) – Management
content:
• Dx: Effusion + Inflammation + Acute onset • Pathogens: S.pneumo, H.influenzae, M.catarrhalis
ALWAYS treat if: Age <6mo | Ruptured TM | Bilateral AOM ≤23mo | Fever >39°C | Unwell >48hr
• First-line: Amoxicillin 45mg/kg/d BID x5-10d • PCN allergy: Ceftriaxone 50mg/kg IM x3d

title: Sinusitis – Viral vs Bacterial
content: • Viral (98%): Treat symptomatically • Bacterial if: No improvement after 10 days OR worsening after initial improvement • Acute Tx: Amoxicillin 0.5-1g TID x5-7d • Chronic (>12wks): Amoxicillin-clavulanate 875mg BID x3wks • ❌ Antihistamines and systemic steroids NOT recommended

title: Sore Throat – Centor Score & GAS
content:
Centor Criteria (1 pt each):
• Tonsillar exudate | Tender anterior LN | Fever >38°C | Absent cough
• Age 3-14 (+1) | Age ≥45 (-1)
→ Score ≥2: RADT or throat culture
• GAS Tx: Penicillin VK 600mg BID x10d • PCN allergy: Cephalexin / Clindamycin / Azithromycin • 🚨 Drooling + Muffled voice → Epiglottitis! • 🚨 Unilateral uvular deviation → Peritonsillar abscess!

title: UTI – Diagnosis & Treatment
content: • MCC: E. coli (75-95%) • Uncomplicated (female): Dysuria + Frequency + Urgency (≥2 sx) • Uncomplicated Tx: Nitrofurantoin 100mg BID x5d • Complicated Tx: Cefixime 400mg daily x10d • Pyelonephritis: Hospitalize if unwell + IV antibiotics • ⚠️ Asymptomatic bacteriuria: ONLY treat if pregnant or pre-procedure

title: STIs – Quick Summary
content:
• BV: Grey d/c + fishy odor + clue cells → Metronidazole 500mg BID x7d
• Candida: Cottage-cheese + pruritis → Fluconazole 150mg PO once
• Trichomonas: Yellow frothy d/c → Metronidazole 2g PO once
• Gonorrhea: Cefixime 800mg + Chlamydia coverage
• Chlamydia: Azithromycin 1g once OR Doxycycline 100mg BID x7d
• Syphilis: Painless chancre → Benzathine PCN G 2.4MU IM once
• HSV: Grouped vesicles → Valacyclovir

title: Contraception – Key Rules
content: • <6 wks postpartum / breastfeeding → No estrogen → IUD / DMPA / Mini-pill • Women >35 who smoke → Avoid combined OCP • Low bone density → Avoid DMPA • Emergency (within 5 days): Plan B (LNG) OR Copper IUD (most effective)

title: COPD – Diagnosis & Management
content: • Dx: FEV1/FVC <0.7 post-bronchodilator • GOLD Stage 3 (FEV1 35%): LABA + ICS combination • Exacerbation: Bronchodilators + Prednisone 40mg x5d + Antibiotics if bacterial • Risk factor: Smoking (#1) + Alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency

title: Headache – Red Flags & Migraine Tx
content:
🚨 Red Flags:
• Thunderclap → SAH → CT then LP
• Fever + Meningismus → Meningitis
• Jaw claudication + tender temporal artery → Temporal Arteritis → ESR + Steroids!
• Focal neuro signs → Mass/Stroke
• Red eye + fixed mid-dilated pupil → Acute Angle Closure Glaucoma
Migraine Tx: NSAIDs → Triptans → Prochlorperazine IV
Prevention: β-blockers (propranolol) | Valproate | Amitriptyline

title: Hypertension – Management
content: • First-line: ACEi/ARB, CCB, or Thiazide • DM + CKD: ACEi/ARB (renoprotective) • Heart failure: ACEi + β-blocker + Spironolactone • Pregnancy HTN: Labetalol, Hydralazine, Nifedipine • ❌ NO ACEi/ARB in pregnancy

title: Type 2 Diabetes – Treatment
content: • First-line: Metformin • CVD or CKD: Add SGLT2 inhibitor (empagliflozin) or GLP-1 RA • HbA1c target: <7% (most patients) • SGLT2 benefits: ↓ CV death, ↓ HF hospitalizations, ↓ CKD progression • ❌ Avoid Metformin if eGFR <30

title: Prenatal Care – High-Yield Points
content:
• Severe Preeclampsia: Hospitalize + Antihypertensives + Corticosteroids (fetal lungs)
• HELLP: ↑LDH + ↑LFTs + ↓Platelets → Deliver!
• GDM First-line: Diet + glucose monitoring → Insulin if targets not met
• Placental Abruption: Painful bleeding → C-section if fetal distress
• ✅ Tdap in pregnancy | ❌ MMR (live) | ❌ Varicella (live)

title: Depression – Diagnosis & Treatment
content: • Screen: PHQ-9 for diagnosis and monitoring • First-line: SSRI + CBT (combination superior for moderate-severe) • Bupropion: Avoid in eating disorders / seizures • Mirtazapine: Good for insomnia + poor appetite • Sertraline: Safest in pregnancy and elderly

title: Pain Management – WHO Ladder
content:
WHO Ladder:
Step 1: Non-opioids (NSAIDs, Acetaminophen)
Step 2: Mild opioids (Codeine, Tramadol)
Step 3: Strong opioids (Morphine, Oxycodone)
• Neuropathic pain: Gabapentin / Pregabalin / TCAs / SNRIs • ⚠️ ALWAYS co-prescribe laxative with opioids • Constipation from opioids NEVER resolves on its own • Overdose: Naloxone for respiratory depression

title: Low Back Pain – Red Flags (TUNA FISH)
content:
🚨 Red Flags:
T - Trauma / age >50
U - Unexplained weight loss → Cancer
N - Neurological deficit
A - Ankylosing Spondylitis (young male, morning stiffness)
F - Fever / IV drug use → Osteomyelitis / Epidural abscess
I - Incontinence + saddle anesthesia → Cauda Equina → EMERGENCY MRI!
S - Steroid use → Fracture
H - Hx of cancer → Metastasis
• ❌ NO imaging in first 6 weeks without red flags

title: Menopause – HRT & Osteoporosis
content:
• Dx: 12 months without menses (avg age 52) • HRT: Conjugated estrogens OR transdermal 17β-Estradiol • ⚠️ Add progestin if intact uterus (prevent endometrial cancer)
HRT Contraindications: VTE | CAD | Breast/uterine cancer | Undiagnosed vaginal bleeding
• Non-hormonal: SSRIs/SNRIs, clonidine, gabapentin • Osteoporosis: Ca²⁺ >1500mg/d + Vit D >800IU/d + Bisphosphonates if high-risk
